description: A versatile and hardy biennial herb, usually grown as an annual. Essential in many cuisines, it comes in curly and flat-leaved varieties. Parsley is rich in vitamins and adds a fresh, clean flavor to dishes.

pinchOutTops.info: Not typically pinched out, but removing flower stalks in the first year is recommended.

november: 🧣 Protect with cloche for late harvests
december: 🧣 Protect with cloche for late harvests

care.sowIndoors: Sow seeds 1cm deep in pots on a warm windowsill (18-21°C). Germination is slow, often taking 3-4 weeks. Soaking seeds in warm water overnight can help.
care.sowOutdoors: Sow in drills 1cm deep directly into pots or prepared ground. Thin seedlings to 15-20cm apart.
care.hardenOff: Gradually acclimatise young plants to outdoor conditions for 7-10 days before planting out.
care.plantOut: Space plants 15-30cm apart in moist, fertile soil. They prefer a spot that doesn't dry out completely.
care.watering: Keep well-watered, especially during dry spells. Parsley likes consistent moisture.
care.feeding: Feed with a general-purpose liquid fertiliser every few weeks during the growing season.
care.harvest: Pick leaves from the outside of the plant to encourage new growth from the centre. Harvest regularly to keep the plant productive.
care.overwintering: Hardy enough to survive winter. Use a cloche or cold frame to keep leaves in good condition for winter picking.

propagation.seedDescription: Small, ribbed, brownish seeds. Known for slow and sometimes erratic germination.

deadheading.info: Remove any flower stalks as they appear in the first year to prevent the leaves from turning bitter and to keep the plant focused on foliage.

seedSaving.method: Allow the plant to flower in its second year. Harvest seed heads once they turn brown and dry. Shake into a paper bag.
seedSaving.seedPodDescription: Seeds are produced in umbels (umbrella-like clusters).

troubleshooting: Slow germination — be patient, it can take up to a month.
Carrot fly — can occasionally attack the roots; consider using fine mesh.
Slugs and snails — especially fond of young seedlings.
Bolting — usually happens in its second year, but can happen early if stressed by drought.
funFact: In ancient Greece, parsley was so highly regarded it was used to make wreaths for winners of athletic competitions and even to decorate tombs.
